pattern: writing python regex/rules to classify messages
action: stop — ask if Claude can judge this itself via tool_use

pattern: rushing to patch a specific classification failure
action: step back — is the classification layer itself the problem?

pattern: classifying pioneers/early adopters in market simulation
action: pioneers = 火高+澤高 (attracted to novelty + willing to rally people), NOT 天高+雷高. This is One Muse's unique insight, not Rogers' original theory.

pattern: calculating target market size for B2B products
action: TAM = population × 2.5% (entrepreneurs) → SAM = TAM × 13.5% (pioneers) × need_ratio → SOM = SAM × budget_fit. Never use total city population as TAM for B2B.

pattern: simulating a B2B product's market adoption
action: distinguish TWO layers: (1) who buys YOUR product = entrepreneurs/pioneers (2) who your CUSTOMER simulates with your product = general consumers. Never confuse the two.
STOP. Fetch real data from government APIs FIRST. Hardcoded fake data wastes all parameter tuning effort. The data is the foundation — bad data = bad simulation regardless of algorithm.

pattern: choosing simulation algorithm without researching existing models
action: DSE first: check academic literature for established models. Bass diffusion (1969, 57 years of validation) exists for product adoption — don't reinvent with SIR disease model.

pattern: running simulation without verifying archetype role classification
action: before running: manually inspect each archetype's energy profile and confirm the role (pioneer/follower/resistant) makes sense for real humans. Data→Judgment→Classification→Simulation. Judgment wrong = everything wrong.

pattern: adding a new module/class/file without checking who will call it
action: stop — first answer: who consumes this output? where does its input come from? wire the connections before building the component

pattern: accepting a DSE/research report claim that function X is never called
action: grep for X in src/ first — reports are hypotheses, code is fact

pattern: making a system fully autonomous without bounds/cooldown/spiral detection
action: design guardrails first (value bounds, cooldown period, spiral detection, batch limit), then grant autonomy

pattern: maintaining the same data in two places (e.g. CLAUDE.md command table + plugin-registry)
action: eliminate the duplicate — generate from single source. two copies always drift

pattern: designing autonomous system that requires human approval before every action
action: replace approval gates with four guardrails: value bounds, cooldown period, spiral detection, batch limit. human approval = bottleneck; well-designed guardrails = safe autonomy

pattern: rushing to complete observation/analysis in one overnight run
action: observations need time to accumulate. don't optimize for 'finish tonight' — optimize for signal quality. slow data collection beats fast garbage

pattern: applying the first framework that comes to mind without questioning its fit
action: stop before using any framework — ask: does this framework's assumptions match my problem? DSE round 1 = question the framework, round 2 = apply after validation

pattern: presenting only fast/aggressive solution options to the user
action: every proposal must include at least one slow/conservative option. user choosing speed is their right; user not being offered slowness is your mistake

pattern: publishing HTML report to GitHub Pages
action: 發佈 HTML 報告到 GitHub Pages 時，必須使用 publish_report 工具。禁止自行 git push 到 gh-pages、禁止自行拼接 URL。工具會執行 publish-report.sh 腳本並回傳已驗證的 VERIFIED_URL，直接用那個 URL 回覆使用者。自行構造 URL 會因檔名不一致導致 404。

pattern: handling sensitive keyword detection in group chats
action: 群組敏感問題 v2：信任夥伴（TRUSTED_PARTNERS）跳過 L1/L2 檢查，只查 L3。所有等級的關鍵詞命中後都會經過 Haiku LLM 語境驗證，日常對話不攔截。L1 需命中 2+ 個不同關鍵詞才觸發。目前信任夥伴：馮士維（8252847174）。
